description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel array, elevated on a single central pole anchored into a grassy base. The array consists of twelve rectangular photovoltaic panels arranged in a 3x4 grid, supported by a robust metal frame. The panels are tilted at an angle, likely to optimize sunlight capture, and the frame is attached to the pole with visible structural supports. The mounting pole appears to be made of steel or a similar metal, providing stability and durability. The base is a small, square concrete or metal pad embedded in the ground, ensuring the structure remains upright. No visible electrical cabling or inverter components are present on the exterior.
description: A pole-mounted solar panel array with twelve panels in a 3x4 grid, elevated above a grassy base and supported by a sturdy metal frame.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the 3x4 solar panel grid and the supporting pole centered beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angle of the panels and the depth of the supporting frame structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the panels, the supporting frame, and the mounting pole.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the panel tilt and the structural supports beneath the array.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward (likely south), orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, ground-based with a steel or aluminum frame and central support pole
attribute_array_configuration: 3x4 grid of solar panels arranged in a rectangular array
